I'm Luan Gonçalves Bosonnet, final-year student at the École Hôtelière de Genève. From the Finance department of the Ritz-Carlton Abama to Geneva's boutique hotels, I'm building the foundations of one dream: my own hotel.

01 · Who I am

About
Me

I'm Luan, born and raised in Geneva, carrying three nationalities: Swiss, Brazilian and Portuguese. That triple identity is not just a detail on a form; it is the lens through which I see the world and the reason I fell in love with hospitality.

Currently completing my Bachelor in Hospitality Business and Management at the École Hôtelière de Genève, I have lived inside very different professional realities: luxury hotel finance, boutique operations, healthcare and VIP event management.

Before hospitality, I spent time studying and working in healthcare. That background shaped me profoundly: it taught me patience, attentiveness and a genuine care for people that no classroom can replace.

🌵 Rooted in three cultures, open to every other

Background in healthcare: Before hospitality, I studied and worked in the healthcare sector. That experience shaped my sensitivity to human needs, my composure under pressure and the care I bring to every interaction.

02 · Career path

Experience

From Geneva's boutique hotels to a 5-star Marriott property in the Atlantic, each chapter added a new layer to who I am as a hospitality professional.

Sep 2025 · Feb 2026

Finance Intern

The Ritz-Carlton Abama, Tenerife ★★★★★ · Marriott
  • Daily Gross Revenue Report covering Rooms, F&B and Spa (Opera)
  • Accounts payable on PeopleSoft and Birchstreet; Income Audit on FIAT
  • Inventory checks across all POS outlets in a 5-star property
Sep 2024 · Mar 2025

Housekeeping Intern

Hôtel de la Cigogne and Hôtel Longemalle · Geneva
  • Daily quality inspections across two boutique Geneva hotels
  • Coordinated cleaning teams, liaison with front desk and maintenance
  • Real-time guest request handling with discretion
Jan 2023 · Jan 2024

Civil Service · Care Assistant

Swiss Confederation · EMS de Drize, Carouge, Geneva
  • Daily support to elderly residents: meals, activities, medical outings
  • Close coordination with nursing teams across the facility
Oct 2019 · Mar 2020

VIP Steward

Genève-Servette Hockey Club · Geneva
  • Guest experience management in VIP loges during matches
  • Access coordination and on-the-spot problem solving
03 · What I built

Projects

2026 · EHG Bootcamp
2nd place

LinVivo

36 hours to build a business: the 10th edition of the EHG Startup Bootcamp, an intensive entrepreneurial challenge to turn an idea into a viable project. With LinVivo, we set out to reinvent textile consumption through a circular, high-end approach: giving a second life to luxury hotel linens by creating unique pieces, with a strong human and local dimension. Awarded 2nd Prize by a jury of innovation and entrepreneurship professionals. I also built the project's website myself.

Visit the website →
2026 · Diploma Thesis
In progress

Business Plan · Viticole de Villeneuve

My final diploma work at EHG: building a complete business plan from A to Z for the Viticole de Villeneuve, as a team. Every discipline of the program comes together in one project: accommodation management, business strategy, F&B, finance, human resources and marketing. Submission coming soon.

2020 · Maturité Santé

Le Deuil Blanc

A research thesis on how to optimally support the relatives of patients suffering from confusion syndrome through the process of "white grief". A deeply human subject, rooted in my healthcare experience.

2018 · TPC · ECG

Smartphone Addiction

My final-year research project at ECG Jean-Piaget, exploring the mechanisms and consequences of smartphone dependency. Written before it became the topic everyone talks about.

06 · Body and mind

Sport

🥋

Martial Arts Journey

It started with kung fu at primary school, four years of discipline that shaped me early. Later came English boxing, which I loved until COVID forced me to stop. Then MMA opened the door to everything, and inside it I found Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu: the art that connects me directly to my roots.

Kung Fu · 4 years English Boxing MMA Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu 🇧🇷
Hiking · Swiss Alps 🇨🇭
Half Marathon · Finisher 🏅
🏃

Challenges

A half marathon. And the famous Coupe de Noël: swimming in the freezing water of Lake Geneva in the middle of December. Some say it's crazy. I say it's character building.

Half Marathon Coupe de Noël ❄️
💪

Today

With a demanding school schedule, I keep the discipline alive through fitness, street workout and hiking. The mountain is my gym.

♪
07 · Another language

Music

Music entered my life early and never left. In parallel with school, I trained my ear and my hands for years: solfège taught me to read the language, piano taught me to speak it.

Today, that classical foundation stays with me, and a new curiosity has grown from it: music production. I have not yet taken the leap, but exploring how music is composed and produced on software is something that genuinely fascinates me.

5

Years of Solfège

Reading, rhythm and musical theory

4

Years of Piano

Classical training and technique

+

Music Production

A genuine curiosity I want to explore one day

A hobby that brings me pure joy. The logic of the game, the quiet strategy of it. No pressure, just pleasure.

Art

Galleries, design, visual culture. I am training my eye for beauty in every form, because one day it will live in the walls, the light and the details of my own hotel.

10 · What drives me

Inspirations

The people and ideas that shape my mindset. A reminder that discipline, resilience and heart matter more than talent alone.

▶
▶ A TED Talk that stayed with me

Charles Oliveira

UFC Champion · Resilience

A champion who kept going through defeat after defeat. His mental strength turned him into a titleholder with the record for most finishes in UFC history. Respected for his big heart and respect toward opponents, in a sport full of trash talk.

David Goggins

Ultra-endurance · Mindset

Living proof of what the mind can endure. He embraced suffering across impossible distances, showing that mental toughness is built, not given. A constant reminder to push past my own limits.

Luke Hopkins

Student athlete · Balance

A student who blends discipline and consistency between sport and studies, taking on Ironman challenges. The proof that you can chase excellence in several arenas at once, if you stay regular and committed.

11 · The vision

My Own
Hotel

Not just a career goal. A life project. A place where design, culture, warmth and genuine human connection converge. A hotel that feels like home, only better. One day, I will build it.

Geneva, my city
